page: Amazon Web Services g7e.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 32 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 16 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz. The CPU includes 320 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Graphics and compute acceleration are delivered via a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in LLM inference tasks across small, medium, and large models for both prompt processing and text generation. It also achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and image processing tests. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression are rated as strong, while standard CPU and database operations fall within the average range.

Given the combination of a high-capacity Blackwell GPU, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and fast local NVMe storage, this instance provides high resource density for demanding workloads. It is highly suited for large language model inference, graphics-intensive rendering, image processing, and high-throughput network applications.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance designed for demanding visual and computational workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 32 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor, 256.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM. The instance delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference across various model sizes, Geekbench single-core execution, and image processing. It also shows strong capabilities in large-block memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression. With 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, the g7e.8xlarge is highly suited for large language model deployment, advanced image processing, and high-throughput graphics rendering workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM for top-tier LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU for top-tier LLM inference and graphics.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized instance featuring a high-capacity GPU, fast DDR5 memory, and local NVMe storage for demanding machine learning and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM
32 dedicated vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8559C processor
256 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and image processing
